summary: CameraBottomSheet implements AC-11/AC-15 cleanly against the Gallery single-shot reference: on-demand permission, DisposableEffect unbind, single-thread executor shutdown, ErrorLog('camera') routing via reportCameraError, and rememberSaveable on showCameraSheet for R8 rotation survival. The rotateBitmapByDegrees helper has solid unit coverage. Findings are all non-blocking: a double-tap race around `capturing` (capture finishes on executor before sheet dismiss completes), cross-thread writes to Compose mutable state from the camera executor, globally-aggressive `provider.unbindAll()` on bind (conflicts with future Task 9/11 shared-sheet work), a minor phantom-attachment path where capture completes after swipe-dismiss, and a handful of polish items (empty-sheet flash while the OS dialog is up, missing AndroidView.update, misleading `onCameraInitError` name).

suggestions.suggestion: `capturing = false` is reset inside the `finally` block on the CameraX executor thread, BEFORE `onCapture` → `animateAndDismiss()` has finished running. The shutter IconButton is gated by `enabled = !capturing && cameraProvider != null`, so between the moment the executor flips `capturing=false` and the moment Compose recomposes the dismissed state, the button is briefly re-enabled on a stale composition. Realistically the `sheetState.hide()` animation masks this, but it defeats the stated intent of the `capturing` flag (doc at lines 79-82 says «Capture button stays disabled while a capture is in flight to avoid double-taps»). Cleaner: keep `capturing = true` sticky on success path — the sheet is dismissing anyway, there's no need to re-arm it. Only reset on `onError` (where the sheet also dismisses via onInitError → animateAndDismiss, so arguably sticky there too). If sticky-on-both-paths is adopted, the `capturing` flag becomes write-once per sheet instance and the cross-thread-write concern below disappears.
suggestions.benefit: Eliminates the double-capture race window without needing main-thread dispatching, simplifies thread-safety story for the flag.

suggestions.suggestion: `provider.unbindAll()` before `bindToLifecycle` is global — it unbinds every use case any part of the app has ever bound to this `ProcessCameraProvider` singleton. Phase 2 has no other CameraX consumer, but (a) Task 9's `AudioRecorderBottomSheet` may legitimately open shortly after, and (b) Task 11 is explicitly chartered to share bottom-sheet host state — a future coexistence with front-camera preview or a video-clip sheet would silently break because of this aggressive reset. Prefer `provider.unbind(previewUseCase, imageCaptureUseCase)` on the cleanup side and skip the pre-bind `unbindAll()`: `bindToLifecycle` with a fresh pair of use cases that are not yet bound will succeed regardless. If belt-and-braces is wanted, unbind only the two local use cases, not all. The DisposableEffect onDispose at line 193 should mirror this (unbind the two use cases only).
suggestions.benefit: Keeps the sheet's lifecycle self-contained and unblocks future multi-camera / shared-sheet work without a future refactor of this file.

suggestions.suggestion: `onCaptureSuccess` and `onError` both run on the CameraX executor (background thread) and write to Compose `mutableStateOf` (`capturing = false`) plus invoke `onCapture` / `onInitError`, the latter two eventually calling `scope.launch { ... }` which is thread-safe, but the Compose state write is conventionally expected on Main. In practice Compose's global snapshot accepts cross-thread writes and the subsequent recomposition is posted to Main, so this works — but explicit dispatching via `Handler(Looper.getMainLooper()).post { ... }` OR wrapping the callback body in `scope.launch { ... }` (the `rememberCoroutineScope` is Main-dispatched) makes the contract explicit and matches how the rest of the Compose codebase handles async state transitions. Same reasoning applies to `onError` at line 249.
suggestions.benefit: Removes an implicit reliance on Compose's global-snapshot cross-thread tolerance; aligns this file with the rest of the codebase where VM state is always mutated from Main or via StateFlow.

suggestions.suggestion: While the OS permission dialog is up (between `LaunchedEffect(Unit)` firing the launch at line 133 and the launcher returning), `ModalBottomSheet` has already entered the composition with `hasPermission = false`, so the body `if (hasPermission)` renders an empty sheet. Material 3 renders the empty sheet with default min-height — the user sees a short blank sheet behind the OS dialog for the duration of the permission prompt. Two clean fixes: (a) only render the `ModalBottomSheet` when `hasPermission == true` — let the permission request happen before the sheet animates in, delaying composition until ready; or (b) keep the sheet but show a tiny `CircularProgressIndicator` placeholder in the empty branch so it doesn't look broken. Option (a) is cleaner and avoids an otherwise-ugly blank sheet flash.
suggestions.benefit: Polishes the first-run UX — no blank-sheet frame behind the system permission dialog.

suggestions.suggestion: `ModalBottomSheet.onDismissRequest` calls `onDismissLatest.value()` directly (no `sheetState.hide()` animation), while `animateAndDismiss` elsewhere DOES animate. Material 3's ModalBottomSheet handles the swipe-down animation itself before firing `onDismissRequest`, so this is functionally correct — but worth a one-line comment at the call site explaining the asymmetry so a future reader isn't tempted to «fix» it by wrapping in `animateAndDismiss`. The `animateAndDismiss` helper is specifically for programmatic dismiss (close / capture / error), not for the swipe path where the sheet animates itself.
suggestions.benefit: Prevents a well-intentioned but breaking refactor later; matches the documented-intent style of the rest of the file.

suggestions.suggestion: The parameter is named `onCameraInitError` but it is invoked from three distinct sites: (1) CameraX provider/bind fail (line 187, description=«camera bind failed»); (2) `ImageProxy`→`Bitmap` decode / rotate throwables (line 240, description=«capture decode failed»); (3) `ImageCapture.OnImageCapturedCallback.onError` (line 249, description=«capture failed»). Only the first is truly an init error. Rename to `onCameraError(description, cause)` for accuracy — the VM method it's wired to is already called `reportCameraError` (good name), only the Composable parameter is drifting. No behavioural change, strictly naming.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a minor ambiguity between the Composable's parameter and the VM handler it feeds.

suggestions.suggestion: `AndroidView` is used without an `update = { }` block. Current factory-only usage works because `previewUseCase.surfaceProvider` is set at view creation and the use-case instance is remembered — no update path needed. But Compose guidance is to always provide an `update` block (even if empty) to signal intent and to avoid a silent regression if `previewUseCase` is ever reassigned on recomposition. Add `update = { /* no-op: surface provider is wired once in factory */ }` with a one-line comment.
suggestions.benefit: Matches recommended AndroidView interop style; prevents a silent binding regression if someone rewrites the remember { } for previewUseCase later.

suggestions.suggestion: If the user swipes the sheet down after pressing the shutter but before `onCaptureSuccess` fires on the executor, `onDismissRequest` runs (sets showCameraSheet=false), but the in-flight capture's callback still executes and calls `onImageCapturedLatest.value(bitmap)` — adding an attachment to the VM even though the user visually dismissed the sheet. The `rememberUpdatedState` reference survives the composition exit (held by the executor's callback closure), so the call succeeds. This is a phantom-attachment UX edge case. Mitigation: add a `var cancelled by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }` flag set in `onDismissRequest`/animateAndDismiss's close path, and gate the `onCapture` call on it. Or accept the edge and document it. Given it only occurs in the ~200ms window between shutter-press and onCaptureSuccess, user-visible likelihood is low; documenting is acceptable.
suggestions.benefit: Closes a rare-but-real UX inconsistency (sheet dismissed yet a photo appears in the thumbnail strip).

suggestions.suggestion: `LaunchedEffect(Unit)` keys the bind coroutine on a constant. If `LocalLifecycleOwner.current` ever changes under this composable (e.g., embedded inside a NavHost child that re-parents), the effect won't re-run and the camera would stay bound to the stale owner. Key the effect on `lifecycleOwner` — `LaunchedEffect(lifecycleOwner)` — so a lifecycle-owner swap triggers rebind. For the current Phase-2 topology (sheet inside a single Activity), this is moot, but it's one character and costs nothing. Gallery's reference uses `Unit` too; not a true bug, just a robustness nit.
suggestions.benefit: Future-proofs against nested NavHost scenarios; matches the parameter's natural key.

suggestions.suggestion: `private val CameraSheetHeight = 480.dp` is a magic number with no comment. 480dp is a reasonable default (half of a typical 960dp tall phone screen) but a KDoc line explaining «covers about 50% of a 960dp-tall phone — matches Pocket LLM reference sheet proportions» or «aligned with Task 9 AudioRecorderBottomSheet to keep sheet heights consistent» would help Task 9 and Task 11 reviewers pick a matching constant.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a magic number; hints future sheets at the intended design proportion.

suggestions.suggestion: The test file's KDoc honestly documents that `permissionDenied_showsSnackbar` and `closeButton_dismissesSheet` TDD anchors cannot be covered by the Phase-2 unit-test strategy (no compose-ui-test). This is consistent with Task 3's precedent and tech-spec § Testing Strategy, and rotateBitmapByDegrees has solid coverage (6 cases: 0°, 360°, 90°, 180°, 270°, -90°). No action needed. If the intent is to later add these via `androidTest` (instrumentation), a `// TODO: cover in androidTest when Compose UI tests land` marker inside the test class — not just the class KDoc — would make the deferred work discoverable via grep.
suggestions.benefit: Makes the deferred Compose-UI coverage searchable by convention rather than by remembering to read class KDocs.

suggestions.suggestion: Task 11 debt: `showCameraSheet` is a per-sheet `rememberSaveable<Boolean>` flag. Task 9 will add `showAudioSheet` as another; Task 11 tech-spec says «shared sheet host state». Consider introducing a small sealed-class `ActiveSheet { None, Camera, Audio }` saved as an `Int` (rememberSaveable supports Int via default saver) once Task 9 lands — that's one less ad-hoc Boolean flag to migrate when Task 11 unifies sheet dispatch. Not in scope for Task 8; flagging as foreseeable debt because task 8's descriptor explicitly names it.
suggestions.benefit: Saves a migration step in Task 11; keeps sheet visibility state central.
